title: bursera dubia
intro: Bursera dubia, also known as the 'Black Myrrh' or 'Jerga', is a plant species in the Burseraceae family, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its resin. The health benefits of Bursera dubia include its ability to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its antiseptic and antifungal properties, which can aid in wound healing and skin conditions. The resin of the Bursera dubia plant is used in various herbal preparations, including tinctures, essential oils, and salves, which can be applied topically or ingested to harness its medicinal benefits.
